text:
So `@haiku`/`@haiku1` would resolve through the **existing bare-slug fallback** in `resolve_recipient`. The replacement is therefore mostly *deletion*, not new resolver code:

| Codename site | Replacement |
|---|---|
| `idref.rs:112` `looks_like_codename` gate in `extract_mentions` | Accept any agent-slug-shaped token (`[A-Za-z0-9._-]`, optionally `@host`). Let `resolve_recipient` decide validity. This is the one real edit: the gate must stop filtering to NATO-codename shape. |
| `chat_write.rs:99-103` "must name a concrete session codename" bail | Drop the `target_session` requirement. Route by `target.pubkey` alone â€” delivery (line 176) already keys on `mentioned_pubkey`. |
| `chat_write.rs:300-308` `find_session_by_codename` step | Remove. The bare-slug step (309-318) + `resolve_agent_pubkey` covers `@haiku`/`@haiku1`. | [...] Issue #99 slice complete. The `@<codename>` chat-targeting path is gone; mentions now accept agent-instance labels that resolve to the instance's selected pubkey, and codenames no longer appear in envelope display.

### What changed per file

**src/idref.rs**
- Module/`Ref::Token` doc comments rewritten: identity = agent-instance label; session_id is correlation-only, never a chat target.
- `session_label` no longer renders `codename (agent@host)` â€” it returns the agent-instance label `agent@host` (degrading to the raw `session_id` only when slug is empty). This is what `format_envelope` consumes, so the envelope "From" line is now codename-free automatically.
- Deleted `token_is_codename`; dropped the `looks_like_codename`/`session_codename` imports (now only `slugify_host`).
- `extract_mentions` gate relaxed: instead of NATO-codename shape it now keeps a `[A-Za-z0-9._-]+` run optionally host-qualified as `label@host`, with trailing `.`/`@` trimmed. (A concurrent intentional refinement also skips `@` preceded by an alphanumeric, so emails aren't mentions â€” coexists fine.) [...] | `idref.rs:112` `looks_like_codename` gate in `extract_mentions` | Accept any agent-slug-shaped token (`[A-Za-z0-9._-]`, optionally `@host`). Let `resolve_recipient` decide validity. This is the one real edit: the gate must stop filtering to NATO-codename shape. |
| `chat_write.rs:99-103` "must name a concrete session codename" bail | Drop the `target_session` requirement. Route by `target.pubkey` alone â€” delivery (line 176) already keys on `mentioned_pubkey`. | [...] - `extract_mentions` gate relaxed: instead of NATO-codename shape it now keeps a `[A-Za-z0-9._-]+` run optionally host-qualified as `label@host`, with trailing `.`/`@` trimmed. (A concurrent intentional refinement also skips `@` preceded by an alphanumeric, so emails aren't mentions â€” coexists fine.) [...] | `chat_write.rs:99-103` "must name a concrete session codename" bail | Drop the `target_session` requirement. Route by `target.pubkey` alone â€” delivery (line 176) already keys on `mentioned_pubkey`. |
| `chat_write.rs:300-308` `find_session_by_codename` step | Remove. The bare-slug step (309-318) + `resolve_agent_pubkey` covers `@haiku`/`@haiku1`. |
| `chat_write.rs:333-348` `find_session_by_codename` fn | Delete (no other caller â€” confirmed by grep). | [...] - Removed the hard `bail!` requiring a mention to name a concrete session. A mention token is now resolved via `resolve_recipient`; on `Ok` it sets the mention, on `Err` (unresolvable) it is silently treated as no-mention (chat is never blocked). The project/work-root mismatch guard is preserved.
- Added `mentioned_label` (the raw resolved token) to the mention tuple and the RPC response; `mentioned_session` is now `Option` (None for label mentions, which route purely by pubkey).
- The codename resolver step and `find_session_by_codename`/`SessionMatch` that I removed were, in a concurrent intentional change by another worker, replaced with a richer `find_session_by_agent_label` (live-local-session-by-label with channelâ†’projectâ†’global scope precedence) plus issue-#98 instance signing; the bare-label `resolve_agent_pubkey` fallback (step 4) remains. The codename path is fully gone and the file builds clean. [...] **The slug/ordinal-label â†’ selected-pubkey resolver already exists and already works**, via two independently-sufficient facts:

1. **Every instance publishes a kind:0 under its display label.** `src/runtime.rs:191-197` publishes `DomainEvent::Profile` with `agent: aref` where `aref = p.instance.agent_ref()` (`src/runtime.rs:171`). `AgentInstance::agent_ref()` (`src/identity.rs:574-576`) pairs the **selected pubkey** with **`display_slug()`** = `"haiku"`/`"haiku1"`. `materialize_profile` (`src/fabric/nip29/materializer.rs:68-79`) stores that label into `relay_profiles.slug`.

2. **`Store::resolve_agent_pubkey(slug, host)`** (`src/state/profiles.rs:56-70`) reverse-looks-up `relay_profiles` by slug+host. So **`resolve_agent_pubkey("haiku1", local_host)` already returns the ordinal-1 selected pubkey** with zero new code â€” and that exact call is *already* step (4) of `resolve_recipient` (`chat_write.rs:309-318`).
 [...] - `/Users/pablofernandez/src/tenex-edge/src/state/profiles.rs` (`resolve_agent_pubkey` â€” the existing slug/labelâ†’pubkey resolver)
- `/Users/pablofernandez/src/tenex-edge/src/state/identities.rs` (identities table; lacks a `(agent_slug, ordinal)â†’pubkey` fn)
